title: Berlin TV Tower Construction - Quick Summary
items: **The Berlin TV Tower was built from 1965 to 1969:** it opened on 3 October 1969, just before the GDR's 20th anniversary.
**It was both practical and political:** East Germany needed broadcasting power, but Walter Ulbricht also wanted a skyline symbol visible from the West.
**The tower reshaped old Berlin:** it rose in the historic Marienviertel area, where war damage and GDR planning erased much of the old street pattern.
**The build was difficult and expensive:** the shaft used about 8,000 cubic metres of concrete and the final cost rose to over 132 million East German marks.
**The meaning changed after 1990:** a GDR prestige project became one of the main symbols of reunified Berlin.
